name: Belladonna Nightshade
description: Belladonna, also known as Deadly Nightshade, is a highly poisonous perennial herbaceous plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. Its association with European witchcraft and folklore is extensive and deeply rooted. The plant's toxicity, stemming from alkaloids like atropine and scopolamine, contributes significantly to its mystique. Historically, Belladonna was used in rituals for divination, astral projection, and necromancy, often incorporated into flying ointments alongside other psychoactive plants. Its reputation as a witch's herb stems from its ability to induce hallucinations, delirium, and altered states of consciousness, believed to facilitate communication with spirits and access other realms. The name 'Belladonna,' meaning 'beautiful woman' in Italian, derives from its use by women to dilate their pupils, enhancing their perceived beauty, although this practice was fraught with danger. Beyond its association with witchcraft, Belladonna held a place in traditional medicine, albeit cautiously, for pain relief and other ailments. Its dangerous nature, however, solidified its connection to the darker aspects of European folklore and the supernatural. The plant served as a potent symbol of both danger and power, a gateway to altered realities, and a tool for those seeking to manipulate the boundaries between the physical and spiritual worlds. Its presence in mythology highlights the complex relationship between humans, nature, and the pursuit of forbidden knowledge.
subtitle: Deadly beauty, witch's herb, altered states.

keyMyths.title: Belladonna and the Witches' Sabbath
keyMyths.description: Belladonna was a key ingredient in flying ointments, used by witches to induce hallucinatory experiences of flying and attending the Witches' Sabbath. These ointments allowed them to transcend the mundane and enter the spiritual realm.
keyMyths.source: Various historical accounts of witchcraft trials and folklore.

sources.author: Nicholas Culpeper
sources.description: Details medicinal uses and astrological associations, though with warnings about its toxicity.
sources.text: Details medicinal uses and astrological associations, though with warnings about its toxicity.

sources.author: Carlo Ginzburg
sources.description: Explores the use of psychoactive plants, including Belladonna, in witchcraft practices.
